danger_signals.visual_form: Armored soldiers on screen, distinct from background
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to unarmored Stone; ranged fire danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive visual battle damage on suit sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Suit integrity degrading; ejection risk increasing

danger_signals.visual_form: Non-armored human characters distinct from soldiers
danger_signals.meaning: Secondary threat; vulnerable to Stone's pistol, indicates facility/base zone
Stone's pistol ineffective; suit mandatory for survival

danger_signals.visual_form: Greyed-out or inactive backpack mount indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Suit has taken significant damage; ranged and special weapons offline

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy soldier entering/piloting player's abandoned suit
danger_signals.meaning: Exposed Stone is extremely vulnerable; immediate threat to life

safety_signals.visual_form: Mech suit sprite standing idle on level geometry
safety_signals.meaning: Respawn point; protection available; mission continuity possible

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing or distinct health restoration items
safety_signals.meaning: Suit damage reversible; full combat capability restoration available

safety_signals.visual_form: Rocket launcher, grenade launcher, mine layer, gravity inverter items on field
safety_signals.meaning: Combat capability enhancement; tactical advantage potential
Increased suit lethality; damage output enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrow passages, vents, small doorways in level design
safety_signals.meaning: Stone can scout safely; most mechanical enemies cannot follow; tactical advantage

safety_signals.signal: Inactive enemies or absence of soldiers
safety_signals.visual_form: Empty level sectors; no armored/unarmored characters visible
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safe zone; suit damage can be assessed; tactical positioning possible

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Six distinct mech suit types (Nitro, Havoc, Prometheus, Spider, Ballistic, Drache) with unique silhouettes; Stone's unarmored form with jetpack
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Liberated/defended facilities; player-piloted suits in level; stolen Axis equipment repurposed; rescue mission objectives (Agent Marissa location markers)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy-piloted mech suits (enemy color variants); armored soldiers; unarmored scientists/technicians; heavy mechanical enemies; asteroid base structures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Axis 5 facility; supply ships; heavily guarded asteroid bases; main attack force positions; dictator Venkar Amon's military infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Three-year active war between Dark Axis and United Earth Government
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Battle-damaged environments; military installations present; fortified bases; enemy patrol presence throughout levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant enemy soldier/mech presence; aggressive patrol patterns; facility defense mechanics; supply chain disruption (supply ships as objectives)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Metal Warriors group operating as Earth's last defense
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player-controlled mech suits as primary visual representation; stolen Prometheus units in group ship defense; multiple suit types available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission-based progression; suit switching mechanics; tactical deployment across nine missions; resource acquisition necessity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Supply ship capture objective; asteroid base resource extraction; power-up scarcity on levels; health/weapon upgrades as collectibles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory supply mission completion; backpack power-up management; suit abandonment when damaged (resource optimization)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Axis military offensive against Metal Warriors
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy attack on player group's main ship; overwhelming mechanical enemy presence; fortified base designs; heavy defensive structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defensive gameplay sections; forced suit switches for defense; escalating mission difficulty; group ship under siege state

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Stone's promotion from lieutenant to captain
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrative progression through mission completion; successful resource acquisition visible in mission results
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased responsibility; command of crew operations; multi-mission strategic objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Initial missions set in space environments; transition to Earth-based operations; asteroid vs. planetary geometry differentiation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission location shifting; strategic repositioning from space to ground operations; Agent Marissa rescue (space objective) to Earth defense (ground objective)

environmental_storytelling.example: Mech suits accumulate visible battle scarring and damage sprites as they absorb enemy fire, communicating combat intensity and suit expendability without GUI health bars
Nitro suit available for speed/mobility scenarios; Prometheus suit specifically identified as defensive unit for ship protection; Spider, Ballistic, Drache suited to specific mission types

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Unarmored Stone displays vulnerability through visual design (smaller sprite, exposed human form); pistol ineffectiveness against mechanical enemies communicates asymmetric threat landscape

environmental_storytelling.example: Health restoration, weapon upgrades, and backpack items positioned as environmental resources; their absence in high-danger zones signals player resource management necessity
Suited soldiers vs. unarmored scientists/technicians visually distinct; their threat levels communicated through suit presence; suit-theft mechanic shows enemy faction technology parity
Heavily guarded asteroid base visual design; Axis 5 structure; supply ship configuration; all communicate military installation security levels through visual complexity and enemy density
Mission location shifts from space environments (Axis 5, supply ship, asteroid base) to Earth operations; environmental visual shifts communicate strategic theater changes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full mobility; all weapons active; backpack power-ups functional; full threat capability
Visual degradation increases; weapon power-ups may disable; mobility may reduce; abandonment becomes viable strategy
Player abandonment likely; Stone ejection mechanics triggered; suit becomes liability
Aggressive projectile fire; suit-specific weapon usage; territory control behavior
Full combat capability; all weapons functional; mission objectives accessible
Extreme vulnerability; pistol only effective vs. unarmored soldiers/scientists; jetpack enables limited mobility; small space traversal for scouting
Visual presence indicates facility security; threat to Stone; potential suit-theft mechanic activation
Indicates technology/research location; vulnerable to Stone's pistol; low threat vs. suited pilots
Immune to Stone's pistol; suit-mandatory engagement; high damage output
Stone can traverse; most mechanical enemies cannot follow; scouting advantage; suit-switching requirement

notes: Metal Warriors communicates world-state and danger primarily through visual damage accumulation, suit availability, and enemy type differentiation rather than explicit HUD elements (notably absent GUI). The faction divide is represented through suit ownership (player vs. enemy-piloted) and facility architecture. The political situation (three-year war, resource scarcity, defensive position) manifests through mission structure (supply acquisition, facility capture, defense objectives) and environmental density of military infrastructure. The Axis threat is conveyed through enemy soldier prevalence, mechanical enemy presence, and fortified base design. The suit damage system is the primary danger communication mechanism—visual degradation replaces numerical health displays.
